summary: Comprehensive testing of Berachain Arbitrage Bot with new system intelligence features. Backend APIs 100% working (20/20 tests pass). Frontend loads correctly with opportunities displaying spread and profit via REST polling fallback. Trade modal and Settings page functional.

minor.issue: Returns 400 'Failed to get quote' instead of error details

integration_issues.issue: WebSocket to wss://bera-arb-engine.preview.emergentagent.com/ws/prices fails - 'WebSocket is closed before connection established'
integration_issues.workaround: REST API polling fallback working correctly - opportunities update every 5 seconds

passed_tests: GET /api/health - returns healthy status with RPC connected (block ~17914671)
GET /api/opportunities - returns 3 ranked arbitrage opportunities with spread/profit
GET /api/engine/stats - returns comprehensive stats with arb_logger metrics (opportunities_found, micro_arbs_found, triangular_found, multi_hop_found, simulations)
GET /api/triangular-opportunities - endpoint exists and returns triangular routes structure
GET /api/multi-hop-opportunities - new endpoint for 4+ token routes exists
POST /api/execute-trade - validates inputs, performs on-chain simulation, rejects unprofitable trades with safety checks
GET /api/tokens - returns 6 supported tokens (WBERA, HONEY, USDC, USDT, WETH, WBTC)
Dashboard loads with all UI components (stat cards, opportunities table, portfolio, gas settings)
Opportunities display with spread percentage and net profit in USD
Trade modal opens correctly with slippage slider, trade details, and execute/cancel buttons
Analytics page loads with 'Connect Your Wallet' prompt
Settings page loads with Trading Configuration (profit threshold, slippage, gas multiplier, auto-execute toggle)
REST API polling fallback works when WebSocket fails
action_items: Fix WebSocket connectivity issue (may be Kubernetes ingress related) - currently using polling fallback
Triangular and multi-hop opportunities returning 0 results - may need price matrix initialization or more token pairs
critical_code_review_comments: BEX router is MOCKED (same address as Kodiak V2) - real DEX addresses needed for production
WebSocket endpoint /ws/prices may not be exposed through ingress - consider adding NGINX/ingress WebSocket upgrade support

success_rate.frontend: 90% (all features work, WebSocket fallback in use)

mocked_integrations.BEX_ROUTER: Uses same address as KODIAK_V2_ROUTER for simulation - MOCKED
mocked_integrations.note: Real arbitrage opportunities show simulated spreads due to mocked BEX

seed_data_creation: None required - arbitrage opportunities generated dynamically from on-chain data
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend APIs working. Frontend using REST polling (5s interval) due to WebSocket timeout. Trade execution requires wallet connection (MetaMask). Triangular/multi-hop endpoints exist but return 0 opportunities - may need more liquidity or price matrix warmup.
